description: Harden the Ink CLI Session UI for SSH, bootstrap, recovery, non-TTY behavior, cleanup, bounded rendering, and documentation.

userStories.title: Add TTY detection and non-TTY behavior
userStories.description: As a shell user, I want the CLI to avoid starting a broken interactive UI when stdout is not a TTY.
acceptanceCriteria: Command startup detects non-interactive stdout/stdin where appropriate
Non-TTY mode exits with a clear documented error or documented plain-output behavior
Behavior is covered by a unit test or scripted smoke check where feasible
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Run after the command is registered.

userStories.title: Ensure clean shutdown and source cleanup
userStories.description: As a terminal user, I want Ctrl+C, /exit, and /quit to restore terminal state and close subscriptions.
acceptanceCriteria: Ctrl+C, /exit, and /quit trigger the same cleanup path
Cleanup closes open CliSessionSource subscriptions and source resources
Cleanup is idempotent when called more than once
Tests cover cleanup with fake source
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add empty and error states
userStories.description: As an operator in a recovery scenario, I want clear messages when sessions, rooms, profiles, or the source are unavailable.
acceptanceCriteria: No sessions state shows how to create a session with /new
No rooms or unsupported rooms state is handled without crashing
No profiles state is handled without opening a broken picker
Source unavailable errors include concise recovery hints and redact secrets
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add large-session and narrow-terminal bounds
userStories.description: As a user with a large session or small SSH window, I want the CLI to remain responsive and readable.
acceptanceCriteria: Transcript renderer uses a documented default row window or viewport limit
Large row fixture test verifies the renderer does not render all rows by default
Narrow terminal width is handled by wrapping or truncation without crashing
Status/input lines remain usable at small widths within documented limits
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Document SSH recovery usage and validation checklist
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want docs and validation steps so the CLI stays reliable as a recovery interface.
acceptanceCriteria: Add documentation for starting the CLI over SSH and using /help, /new, /session, /agent, /status, /clear, and /exit
Docs explicitly state Web Chat remains the main interface and list Web-only exclusions
Add validation checklist covering typecheck, tests, TTY smoke test, non-TTY behavior, Web Chat unchanged, and unsupported feature exclusions
Typecheck passes
